violations.text: Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2025

Working containers (spray bottles) used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. Spray bottles were labeled with the common name of the product. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Utensils were placed in water of 135 degrees Fahrenheit or more. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2025

The sink located on the cookline in the kitchen was observed to be draining slowly and overflowing in a pan on the floor. Plumbing fixtures and drains shall be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2025

Staff member cooking was observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 10/30/2023
The coving in back Chemical/mop storage room located off the kitchen (behind door) was observed to be coming off the wall. Coving shall be repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2023
